title: Xanthophyllum geminiflorum
intro: Xanthophyllum geminiflorum is a species of plant in the family Polygalaceae, commonly known as a shrub or small tree native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of conditions such as arthritis, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Xanthophyllum geminiflorum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Xanthophyllum geminiflorum is commonly used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
taxonomy: Xanthophyllum geminiflorum is classified under the family Polygalaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as milkworts. This plant belongs to the order Polygalales and the class Magnoliopsida. Within the family Polygalaceae, Xanthophyllum geminiflorum is further classified into the genus Xanthophyllum. The genus Xanthophyllum is native to the tropical regions of Asia and comprises around 55 species of flowering plants. Xanthophyllum geminiflorum is a rare and lesser-known species within this genus.
